reasoning: PKGBUILD is standard and safe with pinned HTTPS source/checksum. Both models correctly assessed build script safety but missed AppImage content risks.
coverage_issues: Critical gap: Both models skipped AppImage binary review. This contains the actual executable code with network/filesystem access
Missing review of extracted AppImage contents during prepare() (e.g., squashfs-root scripts)
false_negatives: AppImage binary: Contains the actual application code with network access (inherent risk for cloud-based apps) that wasn't analyzed
Extracted AppImage scripts: prepare() extracts the image but no models reviewed squashfs-root contents for malicious payloads
learnings: File selection: Must prioritize extracted build artifacts (like AppImage contents) that execute at runtime
Prompt improvement: Require models to flag binary risks explicitly when skipping files
Tool enhancement: Implement automatic AppImage extraction for content analysis
Common failure: Models reliably miss risks in binary/archived payloads even when referenced in PKGBUILD
